title: Racosperma phasmoides
taxonomy: Racosperma phasmoides is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a large group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. This species is native to Western Australia and is often found growing in sandy soils. It is a low-growing shrub that typically reaches a height of around 1-2 meters. In terms of its classification, Racosperma phasmoides is often considered to be a synonym of Acacia phasmoides, which is a species of wattle. Further research may be necessary to confirm its exact classification.
